method_atom: Statistical-mechanics analysis of a mean-field Hamiltonian describing many coupled continuous-phase degrees of freedom with quenched structural disorder, using replica/spin-glass techniques to derive a phase diagram over a drive/control parameter and a disorder strength. The machinery yields three generic phases — a disordered (high-noise) phase, an ordered symmetry-broken phase, and a glassy phase in which the variables freeze into an exponentially large number of metastable configurations — and quantifies how increasing disorder shifts the ordering threshold.
object_atom: The phases of the electromagnetic field modes in mode-locked lasers spanning a range of structural randomness, from ordered optical cavities to multiple-scattering random media, controlled by the optical pumping rate (with relevance to other random-Hamiltonian optical systems such as Bose-Einstein condensates and nonlinear optical beams).
brief_reason: The disordered mean-field Hamiltonian with its para/ordered/glassy phase transitions and disorder-shifted threshold is the transferable spin-glass machinery, while the pumped electromagnetic-mode phases of a laser cavity are the optics-specific object.
